codeReview.summary: The feature wires create-task success and failure feedback into the initiator panel, but the success path still does not trigger a task-list refresh and the new tests miss the emitted-event contract that downstream containers rely on.

issues.description: `InitiateReviewPanel` now emits `created` and `close`, but the dock wrapper does not listen for either event. As a result, a successful submission closes only the inner panel content while the dock panel itself stays open and no higher-level refresh path runs. This misses the required behavior to close the panel and refresh surrounding task views after success.

The new success test creates `createdHandler` and `closeHandler` but never asserts that either callback was invoked, so it does not actually verify the emitted-event contract introduced by this change. A regression in `emit('created')` or `emit('close')` would still pass the suite.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission/shared docs still do not record how review panel completion is supposed to propagate through dock wrappers. This feature added emits on `InitiateReviewPanel`, but `src/components/dock_panels/InitiateReviewPanelDock.vue` remains a passive wrapper, which suggests the event-bridging convention for docked review panels is undocumented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `src/components/review/InitiateReviewPanel.vue` emits `created`/`close`, while `src/components/dock_panels/InitiateReviewPanelDock.vue:7` renders `<InitiateReviewPanel />` without forwarding or handling those events; `.factory/library/architecture.md` documents `defineEmits` usage generally but not dock-panel event propagation patterns.

summary: Review failed. The feature adds success/failure feedback in `InitiateReviewPanel`, but the dock integration still ignores the new completion events, so the success path does not reliably close the docked panel or refresh related task views. Test coverage also misses assertions for the newly introduced emitted events.
